atoms.content: Accuracy measures overall correctness, but Precision measures quality (false positives) and Recall measures quantity (false negatives).
Precision is 'Of all the emails I called spam, how many actually were?' Recall is 'Of all the real spam emails, how many did I catch?'
The F1-Score is the harmonic mean of Precision and Recall. It provides a single metric that perfectly balances the trade-off between the two.
For predicting continuous numbers (regression), we use Mean Squared Error (MSE) or Mean Absolute Error (MAE) to measure how far off our predictions are.
A confusion matrix is a grid that visually shows exactly what a classification model got right and what it confused (True Positives vs False Positives).

1. Don't rely solely on Accuracy.
2. Precision = Quality.
3. Recall = Quantity.
4. F1 = Balance.

If you build a cancer detection model, is it worse to have a False Positive (scare a healthy patient) or a False Negative (miss a sick patient)? (Answer: False Negative, meaning Recall is more important).
